session.user:
Update user-facing error/output messages in exec script files. Change "plan" references to "PR" where "plan" refers to an erk-plan (NOT Claude plan mode, erk-plan label, plan.md filename, plan-header metadata block name, workflow names, branch prefixes, variable/function/class names, lifecycle stage "planning", or verb "plan"/"planning").
Read each file first at the indicated lines (±15 lines context), then make edits with the Edit tool.
**IMPORTANT**: Only change user-visible string content. Do NOT change variable names, function names, class names, parameter names, or JSON keys.
1. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py` — search for user_output messages containing "plan". Update strings like "Saved plan" → "Saved PR", "plan created" → "PR created", etc. Be careful not to change references to plan.md (literal filename) or plan-header (metadata block name).
2. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py` — search for output messages containing "plan". Update appropriately.
3. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/close_pr.py`: - Line 51: "Failed to add comment" / line 65: "Failed to close plan" → "Failed to close PR"
4. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/close_prs.py:179` — "Failed to close plan" → "Failed to close PR"
5. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plan_info.py:54` — "Plan # not found" → "PR # not found"
6. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plan_metadata.py:68` — "Plan # not found" → "PR # not found"
7. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py:64` — "Plan # not found" → "PR # not found"
8. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/create_impl_context_from_plan.py:64-65` — "Could not fetch plan" → "Could not fetch PR"
9. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py:92,94,149,249,273` — update plan branch messages to PR branch messages
10. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl.py:245,255` — "Auto-detected plan" → "Auto-detected PR", "No plan found" → "No PR found"
11. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/impl_init.py:73` — "No plan.md found" — this refers to the literal filename plan.md, so leave it as-is.
12. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/impl_signal.py:191,304,383,408` — "No plan reference" → "No PR reference", "Plan # not found" → "PR # not found"
13. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/validate_plan_content.py:69,72,77` — "Plan is empty" → "PR is empty", "Plan is too short" → "PR is too short", "Plan lacks structure" → "PR lacks structure"
14. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/extract_latest_plan.py:44` — "No plan found" → "No PR found"
15. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/exit_plan_mode_hook.py` — search for "plan" references in messages. Be VERY careful here: many references may be to Claude "plan mode" (leave those) vs erk-plan (update those).
16. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py:97,256` — "Duplicate plan" → "Duplicate PR", "Failed to add plan comment" → "Failed to add PR comment"
17. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_fetch_context.py:186,202,209` — plan not found messages → PR not found
18. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_apply_landed_update.py:220,228` — plan not found → PR not found
19. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_evaluation.py:127,137,173,174,189,190` — plan references → PR
20. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_result.py:173,182` — plan references → PR
21. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_plan_header.py:117,134,137` — plan header messages — These reference the "plan-header" metadata block which is a technical term. Check carefully: if the message says "plan-header" leave it. If it says "plan" as a noun for the erk-plan, change to PR.
22. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_update_pr_body.py:273,274` — plan-header messages — similar to above.
23. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_generate_summaries.py:197,303,308,314,332,337,339` — plan issue messages → PR messages
24. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_save_to_issue.py:179,181,182,185` — "No plan found" → "No PR found"
25. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py:116` — "Committing plan to branch" → "Committing PR to branch"
26. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_plan_labels.py:32` — docstring for type def
27. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_pr_description.py:104` — "Fetching plan context" → "Fetching PR context"
Read each file first. Be precise and conservative — only change user-facing strings, not code structure.
session.assistant:
I'll update the user-facing error and output messages in these exec script files, changing "plan" references to "PR" where appropriate while preserving technical terms, variable names, and references to Claude plan mode.
Let me start by reading the files to understand the context of each message:

1→"""Plan save: creates a draft PR with the plan content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-save [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→Options: 7→ --format json|display: Output format (default: json) 8→ --plan-file PATH: Use specific plan file (highest priority) 9→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 10→ --plan-type standard|learn: Plan type (default: standard) 11→ --learned-from-issue INT: Parent plan (for learn plans) 12→ --created-from-workflow-run-url URL: Workflow run URL 13→ 14→Objective linking: use --objective to pass directly, or rely on 15→automatic linking via the objective-context session marker 16→(created by /erk:objective-plan). The --objective flag takes 17→precedence over the session marker. 18→ 19→Exit Codes: 20→ 0: Success 21→ 1: Error 22→ 2: Validation failed 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.validate_plan_content import _validate_plan_content

1→"""Update an existing plan's content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-update --plan-number N [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command updates the plan content comment on an existing GitHub issue: 7→1. Update issue title from plan H1 heading 10→ 11→Options: 12→ --plan-number N: Plan number to update (required) 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage 14→ --plan-path PATH: Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup) 15→ 16→Output: 17→ --format json (default): {"success": true, ...} 18→ --format display: Formatted text 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Success - plan comment updated 22→ 1: Error - plan not found, no plan found, etc. 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk_shared.context.helpers import (

1→"""Batch close multiple plan PRs with comments. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ echo '[{"pr_number": 42, "comment": "Superseded"}]' | erk exec close-prs 5→ 6→Input: 7→ JSON array from stdin, each item: {"pr_number": int, "comment": str} 8→ 9→Output: 10→ JSON with batch results 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Always (even on error, to support batch contract) 14→ 1: Context not initialized 15→""" 16→ 17→from __future__ import annotations 18→ 19→import json 20→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass 21→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypedDict, cast 22→ 23→import click 24→ 25→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_plan_backend, require_repo_root 26→ 27→if TYPE_CHECKING: 28→ from typing import Any 29→ 30→

1→"""Create .erk/impl-context/ folder from plan content. 2→ 3→This exec command fetches a plan via PlanBackend and creates the .erk/impl-context/ 4→folder structure, providing a testable alternative to inline workflow scripts. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan <plan-id> 8→ 9→Output: 10→ Structured JSON output with success status and folder details 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success (.erk/impl-context/ folder created) 14→ 1: Error (plan not found, fetch failed, folder creation failed) 15→ 16→Examples: 17→ $ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan 1028 18→ {"success": true, "impl_context_path": "/path/to/.erk/impl-context", "pr_number": 1028} 19→ 20→ $ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan 999 21→ {"success": false, "error": "plan_not_found", "message": "..."} 22→""" 23→ 24→import json 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 29→ require_plan_backend, 30→ require_repo_root,

" in line) 58→ for line in content_stripped.split("\n") 59→ ) 60→ 61→ details = { 62→ "length": length, 63→ "has_headers": has_headers, 64→ "has_lists": has_lists, 65→ } 66→ 67→ # Validation checks 68→ if not content_stripped: 69→ return False, "Plan is empty or contains only whitespace", details 70→ 71→ if length < 100: 72→ return False, f"Plan too short ({length} characters, minimum 100)", details 73→ 74→ if not has_headers and not has_lists: 75→ return ( 76→ False, 77→ "Plan lacks structure (no headers or lists found)", 78→ details, 79→ ) 80→ 81→ return True, None, details 82→ 83→

29→@click.pass_context 30→def extract_latest_plan(ctx: click.Context, session_id: str | None) -> None: 31→ """Extract the latest plan from Claude session files. 32→ 33→ Searches for the most recent ExitPlanMode tool use and extracts the plan text. 34→ """ 35→ # Get dependencies from context 36→ cwd = require_cwd(ctx) 37→ claude_installation = require_claude_installation(ctx) 38→ 39→ # Extract latest plan 40→ plan_text = claude_installation.get_latest_plan(cwd, session_id=session_id) 41→ 42→ if not plan_text: 43→ click.echo( 44→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "No plan found in Claude session files", err=True 45→ ) 46→ raise SystemExit(1) 47→ 48→ # Output plan text to stdout 49→ click.echo(plan_text) 50→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Exit Plan Mode Hook. 3→ 4→Prompts user before exiting plan mode when a plan exists. This hook intercepts 5→the ExitPlanMode tool via PreToolUse lifecycle to ask whether to save to GitHub 6→or implement immediately. 7→ 8→Exit codes: 9→ 0: Success (allow exit - no plan, implement-now marker present, or no session) 10→ 2: Block (plan exists, no implement-now marker - prompt user) 11→ 12→This command is invoked via: 13→ erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook 14→ 15→Marker File State Machine 16→========================= 17→ 18→This hook uses marker files in .erk/scratch/sessions/<session-id>/ for state management. 19→Marker files are self-describing: their names indicate their purpose and their contents 20→explain their effect. 21→ 22→Marker Files: 23→ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now.marker 24→ Created by: Agent (when user chooses "Implement without saving") 25→ Effect: Next ExitPlanMode call is ALLOWED (exit plan mode, proceed to implementation) 26→ Lifecycle: Deleted after being read by next hook invocation 27→ 28→ exit-plan-mode-hook.plan-saved.marker 29→ Created by: /erk:plan-save command (both new-branch and current-branch paths) 30→ Content: First line is the plan PR number
... [866 lines omitted] ...
403→ f"⚠️ WARNING: Currently on '{current_branch}'. "

session.tool_result:
82→ pr_number: Plan identifier for linking 83→ behind_count: How many commits behind base branch 84→ base_branch: Base branch name 85→ recent_commits: Recent commits on base branch (newline-separated) 86→ run_url: Optional workflow run URL 87→ 88→ Returns: 89→ Formatted PR body markdown 90→ """ 91→ parts = ["## No Code Changes", ""] 92→ parts.append("Implementation completed but produced no code changes.") 93→ parts.append("") 94→ parts.append("### Diagnosis") 95→ parts.append("") 96→ parts.append( 97→ f"**Likely cause: Duplicate plan** - The work may already be merged to `{base_branch}`." 98→ ) 99→ parts.append("") 100→ 101→ if behind_count > 0: 102→ parts.append(f"Branch is **{behind_count} commits** behind `origin/{base_branch}`.") 103→ parts.append("") 104→ 105→ if recent_commits and recent_commits.strip(): 106→ parts.append(f"**Recent commits on `{base_branch}`:**") 107→ for line in recent_commits.strip().split("\n"): 108→ if line.strip(): 109→ parts.append(f"- {line.strip()}") 110→ parts.append("") 111→

171→ repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx) 172→ plan_backend = require_plan_backend(ctx) 173→ remote = get_remote_github(erk_ctx) 174→ 175→ if isinstance(erk_ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) or erk_ctx.repo.github is None: 176→ click.echo(_error_json("Cannot determine owner/repo from current context")) 177→ raise SystemExit(1) 178→ 179→ owner = erk_ctx.repo.github.owner 180→ repo_name = erk_ctx.repo.github.repo 181→ 182→ # When --plan is provided, do direct plan lookup (skip branch-based discovery) 183→ if plan_number is not None: 184→ plan_result = plan_backend.get_plan(repo_root, str(plan_number)) 185→ if isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound): 186→ click.echo(_error_json(f"Plan #{plan_number} not found")) 187→ raise SystemExit(1) 188→ plan_id = plan_result.pr_identifier 189→ else: 190→ # Discovery: auto-fill branch from git state 191→ if branch_name is None: 192→ git = require_git(ctx) 193→ cwd = require_cwd(ctx) 194→ branch_name = git.branch.get_current_branch(cwd) 195→ if branch_name is None: 196→ click.echo(_error_json("Could not determine current branch (detached HEAD?)")) 197→ raise SystemExit(1) 198→ 199→ # Resolve plan from branch via backend (works for both P<number>- and plan-... branches) 200→ plan_result = plan_backend.get_plan_for_branch(repo_root, branch_name)
... [25 lines omitted] ...
202→ click.echo(_error_json(f"No plan found for branch '{branch_name}'")) 210→ click.echo(_error_json(msg)) 217→ click.echo(_error_json("Cannot auto-discover PR without --branch or --pr"))

205→ if branch_name is None: 206→ click.echo(_error_json("Could not determine current branch (detached HEAD?)")) 207→ raise SystemExit(1) 208→ 209→ # --- Discovery: auto-fill PR from branch --- 210→ if pr_number is None: 211→ pr_result = github.get_pr_for_branch(repo_root, branch_name) 212→ if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound): 213→ click.echo(_error_json(f"No PR found for branch '{branch_name}'")) 214→ raise SystemExit(1) 215→ pr_number = pr_result.number 216→ 217→ # --- Resolve plan using PR number (the PR IS the plan) --- 218→ plan_result = plan_backend.get_plan(repo_root, str(pr_number)) 219→ if isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound): 220→ click.echo(_error_json(f"Plan #{pr_number} not found")) 221→ raise SystemExit(1) 222→ 223→ plan_id = plan_result.pr_identifier 224→ 225→ # --- Discovery: auto-fill objective from plan metadata --- 226→ if objective_number is None: 227→ if plan_result.objective_id is None: 228→ msg = f"Plan #{plan_id} has no objective_issue in plan-header metadata" 229→ click.echo(_error_json(msg)) 230→ raise SystemExit(1) 231→ objective_number = plan_result.objective_id 232→ 233→ # --- Fetch objective issue --- 234→ objective = remote.get_issue(owner=owner, repo=repo_name, number=objective_number)
... [15 lines omitted] ...
236→ click.echo(_error_json(f"Objective #{objective_number} not found")) 242→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number} not found"))

112→ timestamp = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).isoformat() 113→ try: 114→ backend.update_metadata( 115→ repo_root, 116→ str(plan_number), 117→ metadata={ 118→ "last_learn_at": timestamp, 119→ "last_learn_session": session_id, 120→ }, 121→ ) 122→ except PlanHeaderNotFoundError: 123→ error = TrackLearnError( 124→ success=False, 125→ error="no-metadata-block", 126→ message=( 127→ f"Plan #{plan_number} has no plan-header metadata block" 128→ " — cannot update learn evaluation" 129→ ), 130→ ) 131→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True) 132→ raise SystemExit(1) from None 133→ except RuntimeError as e: 134→ error = TrackLearnError( 135→ success=False, 136→ error="github-api-failed", 137→ message=f"Failed to track learn evaluation on plan #{plan_number}: {e}", 138→ ) 139→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True) 140→ raise SystemExit(1) from None 141→
... [65 lines omitted] ...

158→ try: 159→ backend.update_metadata( 160→ repo_root, 161→ pr_id, 162→ metadata={ 163→ "learn_status": learn_status, 164→ "learn_plan_issue": learn_plan, 165→ "learn_plan_pr": plan_pr, 166→ }, 167→ ) 168→ except PlanHeaderNotFoundError: 169→ error = TrackLearnResultError( 170→ success=False, 171→ error="no-metadata-block", 172→ message=( 173→ f"Plan {pr_id} has no plan-header metadata block — cannot update learn status" 174→ ), 175→ ) 176→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True) 177→ raise SystemExit(1) from None 178→ except RuntimeError as e: 179→ error = TrackLearnResultError( 180→ success=False, 181→ error="github-api-failed", 182→ message=f"Failed to update learn status on plan {pr_id}: {e}", 183→ ) 184→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True) 185→ raise SystemExit(1) from None 186→ 187→ result = TrackLearnResultSuccess(

Usage: update-plan-header <plan_id> key=value ...", 118→ ) 119→ 120→ # Parse key=value pairs 121→ try: 122→ parsed = _parse_fields(fields) 123→ except ValueError as e: 124→ _fail(error="invalid_field_format", message=str(e)) 125→ 126→ backend = require_plan_backend(ctx) 127→ repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx) 128→ 129→ try: 130→ backend.update_metadata(repo_root, pr_id, metadata=parsed) 131→ except PlanHeaderNotFoundError:

258→ stderr=stderr_preview, 259→ ) 260→ 261→ # Parse title and summary from Claude output 262→ title, summary = _parse_title_and_summary(result.output) 263→ 264→ # Build full PR body 265→ if is_planned_pr: 266→ # For planned-PR plans: preserve metadata prefix, include original plan section 267→ plan_header = find_metadata_block(pr_result.body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER) 268→ if plan_header is None: 269→ return UpdateError( 270→ success=False, 271→ error="plan-header-not-found", 272→ message=( 273→ "plan-header metadata block not found in PR body for planned-PR plan. 164→ plan: str | None = None 165→ 166→ # Priority 1: Check scratch directory for session-scoped plan 167→ if session_id is not None: 168→ scratch_dir = get_scratch_dir(session_id, repo_root=repo_root) 169→ scratch_plan_path = scratch_dir / "objective-body.md" 170→ if scratch_plan_path.exists(): 171→ plan = scratch_plan_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") 172→ 173→ # Priority 2: Fall back to Claude installation lookup 174→ if plan is None: 175→ plan = claude_installation.get_latest_plan(cwd, session_id=session_id) 176→ 177→ if not plan: 178→ if output_format == "display": 179→ click.echo("Error: No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/", err=True) 180→ click.echo("\nTo fix:", err=True) 181→ click.echo("1. Create a plan (enter Plan mode if needed)", err=True) 182→ click.echo("2. Exit Plan mode using ExitPlanMode tool", err=True) 183→ click.echo("3. Run this command again", err=True) 184→ else: 185→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": "No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/"})) 186→ raise SystemExit(1) 187→ 188→ # Create objective issue 189→ result = create_objective_issue( 190→ github_issues=github, 191→ repo_root=repo_root, 192→ plan_content=plan, 193→ time=time,
... [5 lines omitted] ...

101→ raise SystemExit(1) 102→ ensure_trunk_synced(erk_ctx, repo) 103→ 104→ # Sync local branch ref to remote (no checkout required) 105→ user_output(f"Syncing branch: {click.style(branch_name, fg='cyan')}") 106→ git.remote.fetch_branch(repo_root, "origin", branch_name) 107→ checked_out_path = git.worktree.is_branch_checked_out(repo_root, branch_name) 108→ if checked_out_path is None: 109→ git.branch.create_branch(repo_root, branch_name, f"origin/{branch_name}", force=True) 110→ else: 111→ remote_sha = git.branch.get_branch_head(repo_root, f"origin/{branch_name}") 112→ if remote_sha is not None: 113→ sync_branch_to_sha(erk_ctx, repo_root, branch_name, remote_sha) 114→ 115→ # Build and commit impl-context files to branch 116→ user_output("Committing plan to branch...") 117→ files = build_impl_context_files( 118→ plan_content=plan_content, 119→ plan_id=str(pr_number), 120→ url=pr_result.url, 121→ provider="incremental-dispatch", 122→ objective_id=None, 123→ now_iso=time.now().isoformat(), 124→ node_ids=None, 125→ ) 126→ git.commit.commit_files_to_branch( 127→ repo_root, 128→ branch=branch_name, 129→ files=files, 130→ message=f"Incremental dispatch for PR #{pr_number}\n\n{plan_content}",
... [5 lines omitted] ...
